methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $510K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
GenFab Co is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Matt Parlmer that has raised $510K to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 11 robotics and manufacturing peers, including one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private firms such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Given the absence of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with comparable valuations, peer-median triangulation was not applicable. Instead, a stage-progression methodology was used, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage against the $510K raised baseline. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $1.3M. A sensitivity band around this estimate produces a low of $1.1M and a high of $1.5M, reflecting inherent uncertainty in stage-based multiples absent direct peer calibration.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1M to $1.5M (median $1.3M). Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of stage-progression methodology as a standalone anchor and the lack of narrow-set peer comparables in robotics manufacturing at seed stage. A more robust triangulation would require either additional same-stage private comps or refined multiples from the broader sector peer set as the company progresses toward next funding.
